summary: App shell calls into section-4 only to load the archetype roster at boot; section-4 does not own any routes itself, so the contract is narrow.

boot_load.name: loadArchetypes
boot_load.ArchetypeRoster_shape: { profiles: ArchetypeProfile[] } where ArchetypeProfile = { id: string, name: string, preflop_frequencies: object, postflop_heuristics: object, contrast_with_gto: string }
boot_load.purpose: Shell calls this synchronously during boot and stores the resulting roster in a shell-level registry that section-5 reads.

data_passed_not_logic: Section-4 returns a static data structure. Shell does not interpret the structure beyond storing it in the registry; section-4 does not know how the shell mounts modes.

section-1_owns: the registry that holds the result for section-5 to read
section-4_owns: the archetype data and decide() function

test_seam: A unit test calls loadArchetypes() and asserts the returned roster shape and content (>= 6 profiles, all required fields populated).
